items.original_text: Exactly Skscartoon: Race-swapping in these movies is essentially the same as putting black faces on traditional European stories. It’s not fighting racism — it is racism

items.original_text: Starlink Internet access enables people to learn anything and access the global market for their goods & services, improving their standard of living X Freeze: The internet is the one single thing that literally lifts an entire country's GDP Every country that signs with Starlink gets a parallel internet infrastructure Every country that delays gets left further behind

items.original_text: Tired of winning Dr. Catharine Young: American science is at extraordinary risk. NIH has awarded less than half as many grants as it has compared to the past five fiscal years averaged together. 'I thought we were at rock bottom', the official said. 'We are below rock bottom now.'"
items.zh_text: 厌倦了胜利的凯瑟琳·杨博士：美国科学正处于极度危险之中。美国国立卫生研究院（NIH）授予的资助数量不到过去五个财政年度平均数量的一半。'我以为我们已经触底了，'这位官员说。'我们现在比触底还糟糕。'

My casual understanding of AlphaGo was "search-augmented deep neural networks trained with self-play", but I wanted to go
items.zh_text: 有趣！Eric Jang：过去几个月，我一直在从头实现AlphaGo，这是2016年的人工智能突破，它激励我进入了深度学习领域。我对AlphaGo的粗略理解是“通过自我对弈训练的搜索增强型深度神经网络”，但我想深入探究。

items.original_text: RT Brett Caughran A big pivot from Ken Griffin on AI: “Number one is, in the last few months, there has been a step change in the productivity of the AI toolkit. It is profoundly more powerful than it was just nine months ago. And for us at Citadel, that has allowed us to unleash a much broader array of use cases for AI. And it has been really interesting to watch, to be blunt, work that we would usually do with people with masters and PhDs in finance over the course of weeks or months being done by AI agents over the course of hours or days. These are not these are not mid-tier white collar jobs. These are like extraordinarily high skilled jobs being, I'm going to pick a word, automated by agentic AI. And I gotta tell you, I went home one Friday actually fairly depressed by this because you could just see how this was going to have such a dramatic impact on society. When you witness it in your own four walls, when you see work that used to be man years of work being done in days or weeks, it's like, wow, like that's the first time I've seen real impact in our four walls.” This echoes my own experience with agents and the conversations I am having with students, friends & clients. The toolkit has dramatically transformed and it feels like in finance, for the first time, AI is real.

🚨 Agent Swarms Are Multi-Agent Systems That Create Agents with Top LLMs Including - Opus 4.7 for front-end - GPT 5.5 for backend - Gemini Pro for visual understanding - Kimi and DeepSeek for efficiency With Agent Swarms, AI can build complex software, run marketing teams and build an entire business - $0 to $1B using AI
items.zh_text: 🚨 智能体集群是多智能体系统，它使用顶级大语言模型创建智能体，包括：- Opus 4.7 用于前端 - GPT 5.5 用于后端 - Gemini Pro 用于视觉理解 - Kimi 和 DeepSeek 用于效率。借助智能体集群，AI 可以构建复杂软件、运营营销团队并打造整个业务——从 0 到 10 亿美元，全靠 AI。

items.original_text: The Gemini Pro model is rumored to be a GPT 5.5 level coding model 🧐 The catch - it will be more than 50% cheaper at $12/1M output token Gemini will take the lead over both GPT 5.5. and Opus 4.7 on the price-performance curve

items.original_text: Grok CLI has great support for Plugins and Skills. Installing the Vercel Plugin gives Grok cloud deployment superpowers. Watch this creative coding website be generated with Grok and hosted seamlessly on Vercel ↓ http://vgrok.vercel.app
items.zh_text: Grok CLI 对插件和技能有很好的支持。安装 Vercel 插件后，Grok 便拥有了云端部署的超能力。观看这个创意编码网站如何通过 Grok 生成并无缝托管在 Vercel 上 ↓ http://vgrok.vercel.app

items.original_text: Vercel protects your agents' deployments behind SSO like @Okta. Even Production ones, giving you a secure 'intranet' of apps generated with @v0, Codex, Claude, etc. It's all fun and games until your agent gets 𝟺𝟶𝟷 𝚄𝚗𝚊𝚞𝚝𝚑𝚘𝚛𝚒𝚣𝚎𝚍 from the deployment *it just made* though 😂 The solution: 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕 𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚕. By just prefixing 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕 or 𝚟𝚌 you unblock your agent and yourself to easily 𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚕 any URL you have access to within the Vercel ecosystem. Vercel Developers: Vercel CLI now supports native curl commands. Same syntax as 𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚕, but uses Vercel auth to test all of your deployments. https://vercel.com/changelog/use-native-curl-syntax-with-vercel-cli
items.zh_text: Vercel 通过 Okta 等 SSO 保护你的智能体部署。即使是生产环境部署，也能让你拥有一个由 v0、Codex、Claude 等生成的应用的安全“内网”。不过，当你的智能体从它刚刚创建的部署中收到 401 Unauthorized 时，一切就变得不好玩了 😂 解决方案：vercel curl。只需在命令前加上 vercel 或 vc，就能让你和你的智能体轻松 curl 任何你在 Vercel 生态中有权访问的 URL。Vercel 开发者们：Vercel CLI 现在支持原生 curl 命令。语法与 curl 相同，但使用 Vercel 认证来测试你的所有部署。

items.original_text: i appreciate how seriously the team always takes these reports (even when the answer turns out to be 'i got used to the current level of magic and now i'd like more please') Tibo: Codex team is aware of reports of GPT-5.5 performing worse for some users and investigating. We don't have anything conclusive yet and systems are healthy but we will share updates as we go.
items.zh_text: 我很欣赏团队对这些报告的认真态度（即使结果往往是‘我已经习惯了当前的魔法水平，现在想要更多’）。Tibo：Codex 团队已注意到关于 GPT-5.5 对部分用户表现更差的报告，并正在调查。我们尚未得出任何结论，系统运行正常，但我们会随时分享最新进展。

items.original_text: Our new multi-model agentic security system brings together more than 100 specialized agents across frontier and custom models to find exploitable bugs, delivering top performance on the CyberGym benchmark. We used it ahead of Patch Tuesday to help find and fix 16 vulnerabilities. Today we’re announcing that customers can sign up to test it in private preview. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/?v=1
items.zh_text: 我们的新型多模型自主安全系统整合了前沿模型与定制模型中的100多个专业智能体，用于发现可利用漏洞，在CyberGym基准测试中取得了顶尖性能。我们在补丁星期二之前使用该系统帮助发现并修复了16个漏洞。今天，我们宣布客户可以注册参与该系统的私有预览测试。

items.original_text: RT Alex Zhao parameter golf was a blast. 2,000+ submissions. 1,000+ verified github accounts. ideas ranging from quantization and depth recurrence to TTT LoRA, SSMs, H-nets, JEPA, and more. autoresearch made iteration dramatically faster — and led to emergent bulletin boards, issue threads, unofficial leaderboards, and agent-built writeups that helped everyone learn from everyone else. it felt like a glimpse of where interaction with AI is headed: humans setting taste and direction, agents helping explore, coordinate, and share what works. our goal was simple: make ml research accessible to anyone, anywhere. it was amazing to see that happen. full recap: https://openai.com/index/what-parameter-golf-taught-us/ future events: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/openai/form/open-ai-challenge-parameter-golf
items.zh_text: RT Alex Zhao 参数高尔夫太棒了。2000 多份提交，1000 多个经过验证的 GitHub 账号。想法从量化、深度递归到 TTT LoRA、SSM、H-net、JEPA 等等。自动研究让迭代速度大幅提升——并催生了公告板、问题讨论串、非官方排行榜以及由智能体编写的总结，帮助大家相互学习。这让人一窥与 AI 互动的未来方向：人类设定品味和方向，智能体帮助探索、协调并分享有效的方法。我们的目标很简单：让机器学习研究对任何地方的任何人都可及。看到这一切成真，真是太棒了。完整回顾：https://openai.com/index/what-parameter-golf-taught-us/ 未来活动：https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/openai/form/open-ai-challenge-parameter-golf

items.original_text: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc. More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage: 1) raw text (hard/effortful to read) 2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default 3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default ...4,5,6,... n) interactive neural videos/simulations Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444 There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen. TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML. Thariq: http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848
items.zh_text: 顺便说一句，这个方法真的很管用：在提问结束时，让你的大语言模型“以 HTML 格式组织回答”，然后在浏览器中查看生成的文件。我还曾成功让大语言模型以幻灯片等形式输出结果。更广泛地说，在我看来，音频是人类偏好的 AI 输入方式，而视觉（图像/动画/视频）则是偏好的输出方式。我们大脑中大约三分之一的部分是一个专门处理视觉的大规模并行处理器，它是信息进入大脑的 10 车道超级高速公路。随着 AI 的进步，我认为我们会看到一系列利用这一优势的演进：1) 纯文本（阅读起来费力/费劲）2) Markdown（粗体、斜体、标题、表格，对眼睛稍微友好一些）<-- 当前默认 3) HTML（仍然有底层代码的过程性，但在图形、布局甚至交互性上灵活得多）<-- 早期但正在形成新的良好默认值 ...4,5,6,... n) 交互式神经视频/模拟。我认为，外推（尽管技术尚未存在）最终会走向由扩散神经网络直接生成的某种交互式视频。关于“软件 1.0”产物（例如交互式模拟）如何与神经产物（扩散网格）精确/过程性地交织在一起，还有许多悬而未决的问题，但总体方向类似于最近爆火的 https://x.com/zan2434/status/2046982383430496444。在输入方面也有必要且待进行的改进。仅靠音频、文本或视频是不够的，例如，我觉得需要指向/手势操作屏幕上的内容，就像你与一个真人一起站在电脑屏幕旁时所做的所有事情那样。TLDR：人类与 AI 之间的输入/输出思维融合正在进行中，还有大量工作要做，并且需要取得重大进展，远在直接跳入 Neuralink 式的脑机接口等之前。就当前阶段值得探索的内容而言，一个小窍门是尝试要求输出 HTML。Thariq：http://x.com/i/article/2052796100608974848

items.original_text: Claude's Constitution is now an audiobook, read by two of its authors, Amanda Askell and Joe Carlsmith. It includes a Q&A on the writing process, the philosophies that shaped the document, and how it might change as models become more capable. Listen at http://anthropic.com/constitution
items.zh_text: Claude的《宪法》现已推出有声书，由两位作者Amanda Askell和Joe Carlsmith朗读。书中包含关于写作过程的问答、塑造该文件的哲学思想，以及随着模型能力增强，它可能如何变化。收听地址：http://anthropic.com/constitution

items.original_text: Excel has quietly been Turing complete for a long time. Nice to see it now edging toward "AI complete"—SGD, attention, next-token prediction… all in cells. Austin Henley: Excel Copilot one-shotted a tiny GPT-style language model for me inside a spreadsheet: embeddings, causal attention, weights trained with stochastic gradient descent, next-token prediction, and a live slider to watch it learn.
items.zh_text: Excel 长期以来一直悄然具备图灵完备性。很高兴看到它如今正迈向“AI 完备”——随机梯度下降、注意力机制、下一个词元预测……全都在单元格中实现。Austin Henley：Excel Copilot 在电子表格中为我一次性构建了一个微型的 GPT 风格语言模型：嵌入层、因果注意力、通过随机梯度下降训练的权重、下一个词元预测，以及一个实时滑块来观察它的学习过程。

items.original_text: RT Pushmeet Kohli The future of Math is mathematicians and AI agents working together. Very pleased to introduce @GoogleDeepMind's AI co-mathematician: a multi-agent system designed to actively collaborate with human experts on open-ended research mathematics. Mathematicians testing the agent across areas as diverse as group theory, Hamiltonian systems, and algebraic combinatorics have reported impressive results. In autonomous mode evaluation on the rigorous FrontierMath Tier 4 problems, AI co-mathematician scored an unprecedented 48% — a new high score among all AI systems evaluated.
items.zh_text: 谷歌深度思维：转推普什米特·科利——数学的未来是数学家与AI智能体协同合作。非常高兴介绍@谷歌深度思维的AI合作数学家：一个多智能体系统，旨在与人类专家在开放式研究数学领域积极协作。数学家们在群论、哈密顿系统和代数组合学等不同领域测试该智能体后，报告了令人印象深刻的结果。在严格的FrontierMath第四级问题的自主模式评估中，AI合作数学家取得了前所未有的48%得分率——在所有被评估的AI系统中创下新高。

items.original_text: Algorithms are part of nearly every aspect of life, from the physics of the natural world to planning shipping routes. Our Gemini-powered coding agent AlphaEvolve has been accelerating progress over the last year - from quantum and biotechnology to logistics and @Google’s AI infrastructure. ↓ https://goo.gle/4uzfe0C
items.zh_text: 算法几乎渗透到生活的方方面面，从自然界的物理规律到航运路线的规划。过去一年，我们基于 Gemini 的编程智能体 AlphaEvolve 一直在加速进步——从量子与生物技术到物流，再到 @Google 的 AI 基础设施。↓ https://goo.gle/4uzfe0C

items.original_text: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently. kache: you can outsource your thinking but you cannot outsource your understanding
items.zh_text: 这是我最近经常引用的一句话。kache：你可以外包你的思考，但你不能外包你的理解。

items.original_text: Fireside chat at Sequoia Ascent 2026 from a ~week ago. Some highlights: The first theme I tried to push on is that LLMs are about a lot more than just speeding up what existed before (e.g. coding). Three examples of new horizons: 1. menugen: an app that can be fully engulfed by LLMs, with no classical code needed: input an image, output an image and an LLM can natively do the thing. 2. install .md skills instead of install .sh scripts. Why create a complex Software 1.0 bash script for e.g. installing a piece of software if you can write the installation out in words and say "just show this to your LLM". The LLM is an advanced interpreter of English and can intelligently target installation to your setup, debug everything inline, etc. 3. LLM knowledge bases as an example of something that was *impossible* with classical code because it's computation over unstructured data (knowledge) from arbitrary sources and in arbitrary formats, including simply text articles etc. I pushed on these because in every new paradigm change, the obvious things are always in the realm of speeding up or somehow improving what existed, but here we have examples of functionality that either suddenly perhaps shouldn't even exist (1,2), or was fundamentally not possible before (3). The second (ongoing) theme is trying to explain the pattern of jaggedness in LLMs. How it can be true that a single artifact will simultaneously 1) coherently refactor a 100,000-line code base *and* 2) tell you to walk to the car wash to wash your car. I previously wrote about the source of this as having to do with verifiability of a domain, here I expand on this as having to also do with economics because revenue/TAM dictates what the frontier labs choose to package into training data distributions during RL. You're either in the data distribution (on the rails of the RL circuits) and flying or you're off-roading in the jungle with a machete, in relative terms. Still not 100% satisfied with this, but it's an ongoing struggle to build an accurate model of LLM capabilities if you wish to practically take advantage of their power while avoiding their pitfalls, which brings me to... Last theme is the agent-native economy. The decomposition of products and services into sensors, actuators and logic (split up across all of 1.0/2.0/3.0 computing paradigms), how we can make information maximally legible to LLMs, some words on the quickly emerging agentic engineering and its skill set, related hiring practices, etc., possibly even hints/dreams of fully neural computing handling the vast majority of computation with some help from (classical) CPU coprocessors. Stephanie Zhan: @karpathy and I are back! At @sequoia AI Ascent 2026. And a lot has changed. Last year, he coined “vibe coding”. This year, he’s never felt more behind as a programmer. The big shift: vibe coding raised the floor. Agentic engineering raises the ceiling. We talk about what it
items.zh_text: 安德烈·卡帕西：大约一周前在红杉资本2026年峰会上的一场炉边谈话。一些亮点：我试图强调的第一个主题是，LLM远不止是加速已有事物（例如编程）。三个新视野的例子：1. menugen：一个可以完全被LLM吞噬的应用，无需任何经典代码：输入一张图像，输出一张图像，LLM能原生完成这件事。2. 安装.md技能而非安装.sh脚本。为什么要为安装一个软件创建复杂的Software 1.0 bash脚本，如果你能用文字写出安装步骤并说“把这个给你的LLM看”？LLM是英语的高级解释器，能智能地针对你的设置进行安装，内联调试一切等。3. LLM知识库作为经典代码*不可能*实现的例子，因为它是对来自任意来源、任意格式的非结构化数据（知识）的计算，包括简单的文本文章等。我强调这些是因为在每个新范式变革中，显而易见的事情总是在加速或改进已有事物的范畴内，但这里我们有功能示例，这些功能要么突然可能根本不应该存在（1,2），要么以前根本不可能实现（3）。第二个（持续的）主题是试图解释LLM中的锯齿模式。为什么一个单一工件会同时1）连贯地重构一个10万行代码库*并且*2）告诉你走到洗车店去洗车。我之前写过这个来源与领域的可验证性有关，这里我扩展为也与经济学有关，因为收入/TAM决定了前沿实验室在强化学习期间选择将什么打包进训练数据分布。你要么在数据分布中（在RL电路的轨道上）飞行，要么相对而言，在丛林中用砍刀越野。对此我仍然不完全满意，但如果你希望实际利用LLM的力量同时避免其陷阱，这是一个持续的斗争，以建立LLM能力的准确模型，这引出了……最后一个主题是代理原生经济。将产品和服务分解为传感器、执行器和逻辑（跨越所有1.0/2.0/3.0计算范式），我们如何使信息对LLM最大程度可读，一些关于快速兴起的代理工程及其技能集的讨论，相关的招聘实践等，甚至可能暗示/梦想完全神经计算在（经典）CPU协处理器的帮助下处理绝大多数计算。斯蒂芬妮·詹：@karpathy 和我回来了！在 @sequoia AI Ascent 2026。而且很多都变了。去年，他创造了“氛围编程”这个词。今年，他作为程序员从未感到如此落后。巨大的转变：氛围编程提高了下限。代理工程提高了上限。我们谈论这意味着什么。

items.original_text: RT Dan Scavino Jr.🇺🇸🦅 On behalf of Erin and me, thank you to everyone for the love and support we've received. We both had an unforgettable wedding day, and we'll never forget it. We're humbled and grateful to all. THANK YOU! 🙏🏻🇺🇸🦅🚀
items.zh_text: 转发 Dan Scavino Jr.🇺🇸🦅：代表 Erin 和我，感谢大家给予我们的爱与支持。我们度过了一个难忘的婚礼日，永远不会忘记。我们心怀谦卑，感激所有人。谢谢你们！🙏🏻🇺🇸🦅🚀

items.original_text: RT Fox News RECORD BREAKER: “Melania,” a documentary following the first lady in the 20 days before President Trump’s second inauguration, earns $8 million opening weekend — reportedly making it the biggest documentary debut in more than a decade.
items.zh_text: 转发福克斯新闻 破纪录者：《梅拉尼娅》——一部记录特朗普总统第二次就职前20天第一夫人生活的纪录片，首周末票房收入800万美元——据报道，这使其成为十多年来首映票房最高的纪录片。
